source_paper_title: Exactness and Effective Degree Bound of Lasserre’s Relaxation for Polynomial Optimization over Finite Variety
context:
Section 8 (Conclusion and perspectives), paragraph beginning “The geometric condition guarantees that …” (page 21 in the provided text)

“However, Assumption 1 is stronger than the regular sequence property of \{\bar g_1, . . . , \bar g_n\}, see Section C. Indeed, \{\bar g_1, . . . , \bar g_n\} is still a regular sequence if \{g^\infty_1, . . . , g^\infty_n \} has finitely many complex common zero locus. However, the H-basis property may fail if we only assume that \{\bar g_1, . . . , \bar g_n\} is a regular sequence. It would be interesting to investigate the degree bound under the regular sequence condition only.”
title: Degree bounds under the regular sequence condition (without Assumption 1)
latex:
Given integers \(n\ge 1\), \(k\ge n\), \(m\ge 0\), let \(f,g_1,\dots,g_k,h_1,\dots,h_m\in\mathbb{R}[x_1,\dots,x_n]\). Consider the polynomial optimization problem
\[
\min_{x\in\mathbb{R}^n}\ f(x)\quad\text{s.t. } g_1(x)=\cdots=g_k(x)=0,\ \ h_1(x)\ge 0,\dots,h_m(x)\ge 0,
\]
and assume its feasible set \(F\) is nonempty and the complex variety \(V_\mathbb{C}(g_1,\dots,g_k)=\{z\in\mathbb{C}^n: g_i(z)=0\ \forall i\}\) is finite.

For a polynomial \(p\), let \(\bar p\in\mathbb{R}[x_0,\dots,x_n]\) denote its homogenization and let \(p_\infty\) be its highest-degree homogeneous part. Let \(I\subset \mathbb{R}[x_0,\dots,x_n]\) be the homogeneous ideal generated by \(\bar g_1,\dots,\bar g_n\).

Assume only that \(\bar g_1,\dots,\bar g_n\) form a regular sequence in \(\mathbb{R}[x_0,\dots,x_n]\) (equivalently \(\mathrm{codim}(I)=n\)), but do not assume the stronger “no solutions at infinity” condition \(V_\mathbb{C}(g_{1,\infty},\dots,g_{n,\infty})\cap(\mathbb{C}^n\setminus\{0\})=\emptyset\).

Problem: Determine an explicit function \(D\) depending only on \(\deg(f),\deg(g_1),\dots,\deg(g_k),\deg(h_1),\dots,\deg(h_m)\) (and possibly \(n,k,m\)) such that whenever \(f^*\) is the optimal value and \(f-f^*\in \langle g_1,\dots,g_k\rangle + \mathrm{QM}(1,h_1,\dots,h_m)\), one has the truncated certificate
\[
 f-f^* \in I(g_1,\dots,g_k)_{2D}+\mathrm{QM}(1,h_1,\dots,h_m)_{2D},
\]
i.e., Lasserre’s SOS relaxation is exact at order \(D\), under the regular-sequence assumption alone.

open_problem_quote: “However, Assumption 1 is stronger than the regular sequence property of \{\bar g_1, . . . , \bar g_n\}, see Section C. Indeed, \{\bar g_1, . . . , \bar g_n\} is still a regular sequence if \{g^\infty_1, . . . , g^\infty_n \} has finitely many complex common zero locus. However, the H-basis property may fail if we only assume that \{\bar g_1, . . . , \bar g_n\} is a regular sequence. It would be interesting to investigate the degree bound under the regular sequence condition only.”
